''[[The Amazing Spider-Man]]'' (first appearance in films)}} {{Quote|I don't carry an ID with me, you know? Kinda defeats the whole anonymous superhero thing.|Spider-Man to [[Michelle Jones]]|Spider-Man: No Way Home}} '''Peter Benjamin Parker'''''[[:File:Marvel x Sony Agreement.webp|Marvel x Sony Agreement]]'' is the superhero known as the '''Amazing Spider-Man''', or simply '''Spider-Man''', and is the son of [[Richard Parker|Richard]] and [[Mary Parker]]. Orphaned at the age of seven[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XJ7GRIOVzNM ''The Amazing Spider-Man: Behind the Scenes and Beyond the Web''], Peter was raised by his [[Ben Parker|uncle]] and [[May Parker|aunt]] over the course of the next few years. Parker's search for answers about the mystery of his parents and their deaths eventually drove him to investigate the establishment of [[Oscorp Tower|Oscorp]], having learned that their employee, [[Lizard|Dr. Curt Connors]], had been close friends with his father. In [[2013]], Peter Parker’s life changed when he was bitten by a [[Genetically modified spider venom|genetically modified spider]] at [[Oscorp Industries|Oscorp]], giving him powers like super strength, speed, and wall-crawling. Since his powers didn’t fill the emptiness he felt about his parents’ mysterious [[Assassination of Richard and Mary Parker|disappearance]], Peter discovered that his father had once worked with Dr. Curt Connors at Oscorp on [[Cross-Species Genetics|cross-species genetics]], a project meant to cure disabilities like Connors’ missing arm. However, [[Lizard Serum|their experiments]] led to Connors' transformation into the Lizard, who wreaked havoc on [[New York]]. Peter managed to stop him, with help from [[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen Stacy]] and her father, [[George Stacy|Captain Stacy]], who died in the [[Battle at Oscorp Tower|fight]]. Devastated, Peter promised to protect Gwen by staying away from her, but their bond made it hard to keep that promise. [[2014|Over time]], Peter became a media sensation as Spider-Man, even though his crime-fighting often clashed with the police. Haunted by visions of Captain Stacy, he and Gwen eventually broke up. At the same time, his childhood friend [[Green Goblin|Harry Osborn]] re-entered his life, desperate for a cure to a [[Retroviral Hyperplasia|genetic illness]] that only Spider-Man’s blood might heal. Peter refused, fearing the dangers, leading Harry to team up with [[Electro]], a former Oscorp employee turned supervillain. With Gwen’s help, Peter defeated Electro, but Harry’s attack on Peter resulted in Gwen’s tragic death. Broken, Peter abandoned his role as Spider-Man, drowning in grief and guilt, until he found strength in Gwen’s final words to keep going by defeating the [[Rhino]], though he would still be haunted by Gwen's death for the [[2020s|next decade]]. [[2020s|Ten years after Gwen's death]], Peter was transported into an [[List of Alternate Universes|alternate universe]]As depicted in ''{{WPS|Spider-Man: Far From Home|Spider-Man: Far From Home}}'' (2019), where he teamed up with [[Spider-Man/Sacred Marvel|two]] [[Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man|other]] alternate [[Spider-Men]] to cure villains from his own world, including alternate versions of [[Lizard/Variant Lizard|Connors]] and [[Electro/Variant Electro|Electro]]. In the process, he saved one of the other Spider-Men’s [[Michelle Jones|loved ones]], giving him closure over Gwen’s loss. Returning to his [[Earth-120703|own universe]], Peter felt a renewed sense of peace, ready to keep fighting as Spider-Man. == Biography == ===Early life=== Peter was born to [[Richard Parker|Richard]] and [[Mary Parker|Mary]] Parker in August, 1995. In his early childhood years, Peter lived a very happy life with them, known to prefer sandwiches without crust, and to sleep with a little light on at night. Peter also met and befriended [[Green Goblin|Harry Osborn]] at his primary school, and the two spent much of their time together. ==== Parents' Disappearance==== {{Quote|You'll stay with Aunt May... and Uncle Ben for a little while.
"I wanna go with you.|[[Richard Parker]] and Peter Parker|The Amazing Spider-Man}}[[File:EmDXLxkW0AASAmZ.jpg|thumb|Peter's last moment with his father.]] One day, while playing hide-and-seek with his father, Peter found his father's office ransacked. Realizing something was wrong, he called out for his father, who surveyed the scene before frantically packing as Peter was carried away from the office by his mother. After his parents had what they needed, they drove Peter to his [[Ben Parker|Uncle Ben]] and [[May Parker|Aunt May's]] house. On the way, Peter noticed his father frantically fumbling with his [[Richard Parker's breifcase|briefcase]] and flipping through the notebook inside. Once they had arrived, Peter stood and watched as his parents quietly talked with his aunt and uncle. His aunt noticed him watching and quietly smiled at him; however, Peter remained silent. His father and mother soon came to him to say goodbye, with his father telling him that he would be staying with his aunt and uncle. When Peter protested, his father merely kissed him on the head before his mother crouched in front of him, stroking his hair and telling his aunt about how he liked to eat and sleep before she was ushered out by his father. Peter was simply told by his father to be good, and the pair left as his aunt held him gently. Peter's parents were later reported to have died in a plane crash, leaving him an orphan. ====Midtown Science High School Student ==== [[File:Peter Parker.png|thumb|Teen Peter.]] For the next few years of his life, Peter was raised by Ben and May Parker. The bond he had with them became so strong that he began to consider them his real parents, as his resentment grew towards his real father, who he believed had abandoned him out of selfishness. At some point in his childhood, he began wearing braces. At the age of eleven, Peter was told by [[Green Goblin|Harry]] that his [[Norman Osborn|father]] had decided to send him to a boarding school. The two said their goodbyes, and from that time Peter spent his childhood friendless. At some point, Peter had his braces removed, and developed an interest in skateboarding and photography. Because of this, his aunt and uncle bought him several skateboards, as well as a camera. By the age of seventeen, Peter was attending [[Midtown Science High School|Midtown High School]] during the year [[2013]], where he was constantly bullied by [[Flash Thompson]], as well as becoming extremely unpopular for his social status as a nerd. He was also the school's photographer, taking high quality photos of several school teams such as the debate team. At some point, he also developed a crush on [[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen Stacy]], who was considered the most attractive, popular, and intelligent girl in the school, but never had the courage to talk to her. ===Learning About Curt Connors=== ''To be added'' ====[[Transformation of Peter Parker|Gaining Super Powers]]==== {{RewriteSection}} Gwen, who was the group's guide and assistant to Connors, recognized Peter very quickly. She allowed him to stay, as long as he stayed out of trouble. Trying to find out more about his father's work, Peter snuck into a lab where genetically altered spiders were stored. As he watched them, one of them pierced to stick to his neck and bit him. Peter took a train home, and fell asleep. It was there that Peter's abilities began to show, on the train a man puts a bottle of beer on his head, and Peter involuntarily wakes up discovering that he could stick to walls as well as possessing enhanced strength and agility, which gave him led to an awkward situation with some of the passengers on the train. [[File:Amazing Spider-man 07.jpg|thumb|Peter's new abilities make him stick to the top of the train|239x239px]] Frightened by his new abilities, he tries to investigate the symptoms of the spider bite, but was unable to find the spider that bit him, as it was a genetic mutation. He decides to visit [[Lizard|Dr. Connors]] at his house, revealing that he is Richard's son. After a brief discussion, Peter gives Connors the equation to complete a formula on limb regeneration, which he learned from studying his father's notes. ====Humiliating Flash==== {{Quote|Take it.|Peter teasing to give the Basketball to [[Flash Thompson|Flash]]|The Amazing Spider-Man}} ''To be added'' ===Becoming Spider-Man=== ====[[Assassination of Ben Parker|Uncle's Death]]==== [[File:Not choice.... but responsibility.png|thumb|250x250px|Parker argues with [[Ben Parker|Uncle Ben]]]]{{Quote|[[Richard Parker|He]] believed that if you could do good things for other people, you had a moral obligation to do those things! That's what's at stake here. Not choice. Responsibility.|[[Ben Parker|Uncle Ben]] to Peter Parker|The Amazing Spider-Man}} When Peter returns home he finds Ben waiting for outside, as forget to pick up [[May Parker|May]], and has him apologize to her, despite May insisting they she can home by herself, [[Ben Parker|Ben]] then tells Peter about he is a lot like his [[Richard Parker|father]], and his father's belief in helping others when you can. He then gets into argument with his uncle about him not being responsible, which leads to Peter storming out of the house, shattering the glass on the door after he slams it. Peter then attempts to a chocolate milk from a convenience store but he 2 cents short, so the [[List of Minor Characters#The Amazing Spider-Man|clerk]] refuses to let him buy the {{WPS|chocolate milk|chocolate milk}}. An angry Peter then witnesses a [[Dennis Carradine|thief]] robbing the store, then the man the gives him the chocolate milk he couldn't buy. After the clerk realizes he was robbed, he asks Peter for some help, but he refuses. However, he then finds the thief accidentally shot Ben, while he out looking for Peter and he tried to stop the thief. He then returns home with the police talking to May about the thief then Peter asks for the wanted poster of the thief, which the officer gives him, before letting Peter one detail about the thief, he has a star tattooed on his left arm. He finds a Ben left a message on his phone, but he is too depressed to hear it. The next day at Midtown High, Peter is consulted by both Flash and Gwen, about Ben's death. ====Crime-Fighting as a Vigilante ==== ===== Responsibility ===== [[File:Prototype Spider-Man.jpg|thumb|Parker becoming a vigilante|left|231x231px]]{{RewriteSection}} Later that night when Peter decides to look for the thief, he finds a man, ''matching the description'' of the thief, beating up his girlfriend and accuses him of being Ben's killer, then starts beating him up. Then he attempts to flee from a group of thugs using powers being confronting the man, before almost letting fall off a building until he discovers that he doesn't a have a star tattoo his arm. He then lets the man live before falling through the roof into a wrestling ring, while the man yells to Peter he has send his face. [[File:58370377.png|thumb|204x204px|Parker is prepared to become Spider-Man]] Peter then sees a luchador-wrestling poster, which gives an idea to create a mask to hide identity. He creates a [[Vigilante suit|mask]] to go after criminals that match the description of the thief, and then he makes mechanical devices to shoot a biocable web made by [[Oscorp Industries|Oscorp,]] and later adds a {{WPS|Spandex|spandex}} [[Spider-Man Suit|suit]]. ====Stopping a Car Thief==== [[File:Screenshot (795).png|left|thumb|240x240px|Spider-Man meets a [[List of Minor Characters#The Amazing Spider-Man|car thief]]]] One night he while captures a car thief that also match the description of the thief, he confronted the [[NYPD]], who looking arrest him for his vigilante activities, but he manages from the officers led by Gwen's father Captain [[George Stacy]], although he does get a bruise on his face from being hit by a bus while escaping from them. After returning home he is then confronted by [[May Parker|May]] being home late and picking up the eggs she asked him to get before going after the car thief. She then sees the bruise on his face and warns Peter that secrets have a cost. The next day at school, Gwen invites Peter to her apartment for dinner that, which he accepts. ====Dinner with the Stacies==== [[File:Peter with Stacies (ASM1).png|left|thumb|249x249px|Parker claims Spider-Man is a 'hero']] When Peter accepts Gwen's invitation to dinner at her house, Peter meets the Stacy family. Over dinner, Peter argues with George, claiming that "Spider-Man" is a hero, not a vigilante. This annoys George, who insists that he is nothing more than a criminal in a mask. After a brief argument, he and Gwen go to "get some air" on the rooftop. Here, Gwen knew he is Spider-Man, then kissing each other, but soon leaves in response to a police report at the [[Williamsburg Bridge|Williamsburg Bridge.]] ====Saving the Innocents==== [[File:Spidey and Jack.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Spider-Man saving a [[Jack|boy]]]] On the bridge, Peter is confronted by a large reptilian creature known as the Lizard. Peter manages to intercept the villain, but is unable to fight him off, as he rescues a little boy from a burning car while the lizard escapes. It is there that Peter takes on the name of Spider-Man and the public begins to know him widely. Spider-Man would be later shown in a "Wanted" picture by the [[NYPD]].[[:File:NYPDwanted.jpg|NYPD's "Wanted" Picture of Spider-Man]] ====[[Fight in the Sewers|Fighting the Lizard]]==== [[File:Screenshot (819).png|left|thumb|240x240px|Spider-Man prepares to take pictures of [[Lizard]]]] The next day, Peter goes to meet with [[Lizard|Dr. Connors]] at [[Oscorp Industries|Oscorp]], realizing very quickly that Connors had mutated himself into the Lizard. He tries to warn Captain Stacy about it, but he doesn't believe his claims. Peter later decides to track the lizard in the sewers, and also get photos of him for a cash reward from [[The Daily Bugle|''The Daily Bugle'']], creating giant webs securing him and his camera.[[File:Sewer gallery.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Spider-Man meeting [[Lizard]]]] Upon finding him he is attacked by the Lizard and critically injured. When Peter escapes from him he leaves his camera in the sewer, the lizard finds it and reads a label that says 'Property of Peter Parker', thereby discovering Spider-Man's identity. ====[[Battle of Midtown High|Battle in Midtown High]] ==== {{Quote|[[Lizard|Somebody]]'s been a bad Lizard.|Spider-Man to the [[Lizard]]|The Amazing Spider-Man}} ''To be added'' ====[[Battle at Oscorp Tower|Final Confrontation with the Lizard]]==== [[File:Spider-Man shows himself.png|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man shows himself to [[George Stacy|Captain Stacy]]|left]]On his way, Peter is attacked by the [[NYPD]] and is hit by an electrified dart, briefly rendering him unconscious. [[George Stacy|Captain Stacy]] unmasks him, but Peter is awakened by his [[Spider-Sense|spider-sense]], and attacks Stacy before he can see who he is. After covering the officers' eyes with his webs and easily defeating them, he is forced to reveal to Captain Stacy who he really is. George realizes that Peter is really trying to protect the [[New York City|city]], and allows him to leave. However, on his way, an officer shoots him in the leg. [[File:Spider-Man Web Swinging.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man swings through the help of cranes]] A weakened Spider-Man is then assisted by crane operators, as one of them was the father of the boy he saved on the bridge. The operators move their cranes in a way that creates a straight line towards [[Oscorp]]. [[File:Spider-Man captured.png|thumb|251x251px|Spider-Man is restrained by [[Lizard]]|left]] At Oscorp, Spider-Man arrives to fight the Lizard, but is restrained by his tail. He crushes his [[Web-Shooters]] and removes his mask, mocking him for losing his [[Richard Parker|par]][[Mary Parker|ents]] and his [[Ben Parker|uncle]]. Captain Stacy arrives and helps Spider-Man by shooting the Lizard, Peter uses {{WPS|Liquid nitrogen|liquid nitrogen}} to freeze the Lizard, making him more vulnerable. He then manages to get to the chemical device and switches the reptile serum with the [[Reptillian Antidote|antidote]] Gwen had made.[[File:Spider-Man (TASM).png|thumb|251x251px|Spider-Man talks to [[George Stacy|Captain Stacy]]]]The Lizard eventually critically wounds Captain Stacy and attempts to stop Peter, but fails, and the antidote cloud reverts the Lizard back into Curt Connors. Peter, who's web-shooters were broken, was about to fall down. Thankfully, Connors saved him. Peter watched Connors crying for his failed expiriment but shortly Connors told Peter to check the Captain. When Captain Stacy is dying, Peter is forced to promise him to stay away from Gwen, to keep her out of harm's way. Peter sneakily attends Captain Stacy's funeral, and ignores Gwen for the next few days at her father's request. Peter finally tells Gwen that her relationship is over, but she soon realizes that her father was the reason for it. ==== Breaking up with Gwen==== The next day at school, a teacher tells Peter not to make promises he can't keep, in response to his being late. Sitting behind Gwen, he whispers to her in a low voice that "promises that cannot be kept are the best ones", revealing that he was going to return to her, after hearing [[Ben Parker|Uncle Ben]]'s voice message and realizing that he too. He has a responsibility to Gwen. ==== Further Crime-Fighting==== A couple of months after stopping the [[Lizard]] Peter told [[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen]] that he had been hired by [[J. Jonah Jameson]] of the ''[[The Daily Bugle|Daily Bugle]]'' to take photos of Spider-Man. With this in mind Gwen told Peter to make a new costume that had more expression suddenly on the police radio Peter heard about a mysterious power outage and so went to investigate. arriving before the cops Spider-Man sees some criminals carrying boxes out of a van and so pursues them. As he enters the building he quickly figures out that the men lead him into a trap when one of them activates a large centrifuge which Spider-Man falls into though he manages to survive thanks to quick thinking and manages to capture the rest of the thugs. Later while on a subway Peter decides to take Gwen's advice to heart and begins to draw up [[Spider-Man Suit#The New Spider-Man Suit|designs for a new costume]].''[[The Amazing Spider-Man Cinematic Infinite Comic|The Amazing Spider-Man Cinematic Infinite Comic Vol. 1.1]]'' ===Graduation Day=== [[File:The-Amazing-Spider-Man-2 Opening.png|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man arrives to fight crime]] {{Quote|Hey, you're not a nobody. You're a somebody!|Spider-Man to [[Electro|Max Dillon]]|The Amazing Spider-Man 2}} Approximately [[2014|one year]] after his [[Battle at Oscorp Tower|fight]] with [[Lizard|Dr. Connors]], Spider-Man is first seen free falling from the sky, in a new [[Spider-Man Suit|suit]]. He begins web swinging, now showing drastic improvement in his web swinging skills, along with upgraded web shooters. He catches notice of a hijacked [[Oscorp Industries|Oscorp]] truck that contains plutonium, through the police radio he has hooked up in his mask. [[File:Mr Criminal.png|left|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man confronts [[Rhino|Aleksei Sytsevitch]]]] He gives chase, also showing an increased sense of humor. He knocks on the drivers door and says,''"Hello? Mister criminal!"'' ([[Aleksei Sytsevich]] rolls down window) ''"Hey there, I'm Spider-Man. You can call me web-head, you can call me amazing, just don't call me late for dinner, get it?"'' When a citizen named [[Electro|Max Dillon]] was in trouble, Spider-Man Dillon from an incoming taxi. [[File:640px-Maxwell Dillon and Spider-Man.jpg|thumb|256x256px|Spider-Man encourages [[Electro|Max Dillon]]]] He tells Max, after Max says he's a nobody, that he needs him, and that Max is his eyes and ears, before swinging away, much to Dillon's delight. He manages to nab two of the criminals, but cannot get Sytsevich as easily. He gets a call from [[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen]], telling him he is late for his {{WPS|Graduation|graduation}}, as it already started. He sees a vision of [[Captain Stacy]] while talking to Gwen, which makes him remember his promise. He tells Gwen he will be there soon, and hangs up. [[File:Amazesequel04.jpg|left|thumb|260x260px|Spider-Man succesfully stops [[Aleksei Sytsevich]]]] Now a little frustrated, Spider-Man catches up with Sytsevich and says impatiently, ''"I told you, I am running a bit late!"'' Right then, Sytsevich crashes into a bus, forcing Spider-Man into action to save an [[List of Minor Characters#The Amazing Spider-Man 2|elderly man]] who is crossing with a [[List of Minor Characters#The Amazing Spider-Man 2|little girl]]. He catches up to Sytsevich who is attempting to escape while Spider-Man is distracted. He dodges Sytsevich's bullets, then webs him up while whistling the {{WPS|Spider-Man (theme song)|original Spider-Man cartoon theme song}}. He makes it to graduation on time, almost forgetting to take off his mask. Peter kisses Gwen on stage before giving the principal a high-five. ====Break Up with Gwen==== [[File:Ym9NVc8.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Parker breaking up with [[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen Stacy]]]]After graduation, [[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen]] invites Peter to come eat dinner at a restaurant with her and her [[Helen Stacy|fa]][[Simon Stacy|m]][[Howard Stacy|il]][[Philip Stacy|y]]. He promises to come, but when he arrive, he cannot bring himself to come in the restaurant. Gwen spots him, and comes outside to ask what is wrong. He tells her about the visions he's been having of her [[George Stacy|father]]. After telling her that although he loves her, he can't keep her in danger of dating the man who is Spider-Man. Gwen, who becomes mighty frustrated, breaks up with Peter and leaves. Peter reluctantly leaves. ====Acknowledged by People==== It is then shown that a news station states that Spider-Man appearances are increasing, which reveals Peter has allowed Spider-Man to take over a majority of his life. Scenes are shown of Peter returning home after patrolling [[New York City|NYC]] as Spider-Man, each time his suit is either covered in bird crap, wet, burnt, or torn. At one point, Spider-Man is at a pharmacy, as he is sick with a cold. A man is attempting to rob the pharmacy. Peter quickly changes into Spider-Man and webs up the robber. The clerk gets excited and exclaims,''" Hey, you're Spider-Man!"'' Spidey replies with''," I'm Spiduh-Mun."'' His nose is stuffed up, so the clerk doesn't understand him as Spider-Man buys the medicine he needs, while still wearing his coat and cap with his suit. ====Laundry Problems==== {{Quote|Last time you did it, you turned everything [[Spider-Man Suit|blue and red]], so no."
"That was a mistake. Because I washing the [[United States of America|American]] flag. My... Can I just please just...?"
"No one washes a flag.|[[May Parker]] and Peter Parker|The Amazing Spider-Man 2}} ''To be added.'' ==== Reunion with Harry==== [[File:Andrew spidey unmasked.jpg|left|thumb|Peter having fun with his reunited friend, [[Green Goblin|Harry Osborn]]]] After Peter hears of his old friend, Harry Osborn, returning to NYC after the death of Norman Osborn, Peter drops by during one of Harry's meetings. Although Harry refuses to hang out with Peter at first, he eventually decides to come with Peter. They are seen walking at a park, trying to catch up for lost years. When Harry asks Peter what he's been up to, Peter says he does some "web designs". When Peter asks Harry about where he went after all those years, Harry explains that his father, Norman, had sent Harry away to boarding school to, as Norman said,"Keep Harry safe". Peter and Harry eventually strike up a conversation on girls. Peter asks Harry if he has a girlfriend. Harry replies with no, but when Harry asks Peter, he says it's "complicated". They start skipping stones across the pond. At one point, Peter skips a stone incredibly far. Harry says,"Whoa! Nice arm!" Peter replies with,"It's just in the wrist, buddy!" === [[The Night Gwen Stacy Died|Conflict with Electro]]=== ====[[Duel at Times Square|First Confrontation with Electro]]==== [[File:Asmcap1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man saves a [[List of Minor Characters|policeman]]]]{{Quote|Yo, [[Electro|Sparkles]]!"
"You don't remember me?"
"Of course I remember you! You're my eyes and ears! Uhh, what's your name again? Uhh, do I know it? I know it! Don't tell me...|Spider-Man and [[Electro]]|The Amazing Spider-Man 2}} Peter arrives as Spider-Man downtown at the center of [[New York City|New York]] to see Max now as [[Electro]]. Max, who is attempting to absorb the electricity of downtown NY, is observing himself on the monitors, when he sees Spider-Man arrive. He marvels at first at seeing Spider-Man. He says "It's you!" However, due to his unnatural blue appearance, Spider-Man does not recognize Max. Spider-Man replies with "Who are you?" Max tells him that Spider-Man called him his eyes and ears. This seems to poke at a brain cell, where Spider-Man remembers his face, but not his name. He tells everybody that they know each other and that their fine. Max becomes distressed when he starts talking about how he doesn't know what happened to him and becomes more upset when Spider-Man does not remember his name. [[File:Spider-man top of car.png|left|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man reunites with [[Electro|Max Dillon]]]] Spider-Man tells Max that he knows he does not want to hurt anybody, and just wants to help Max. However, a police officer receives orders that if Max takes another step, he'll need to shoot him. Spider-Man does not know this, so he is just as surprised as Max when the police shoot at Max. [[File:Ss-amazing-spider-man-08j.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man uses with [[Web-Shooters]]]] Becoming enraged, Max retaliates. Spider-Man tries to stop him, but his left web-shooter ends up being damaged by Max and Spider-Man then uses just the right web-shooter to save the civilians from an incoming car and from touching the electrified railings. Max begins to hear voices in his head, chanting that Spider-Man lied to him. Feeling enraged, he accuses Spider-Man of being selfish and lying to him. Spider-Man denies this and tries to convince him that he is trying to help, but Max refuses to believe him. He then violently attacks Spider-Man and the public and attempts to absorb all the power in Times Square, but Spider-Man eventually blasts him with water. Spider-Man gives the firemen back their hose, after swinging it like a gunslinger, and saying, "Gentlemen, it's been a pleasure, serving with you". Spider-Man then swings over to [[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen]] and says, "England? Really?", then swings away. ====Upgrading his Web-Shooters==== [[File:Asm2cap33.jpg|left|thumb|250x250px|Peter decorates his room]] {{Quote|Welcome to Dr. [[Jallings]] Science Investigator. Today, we're gonna be talking about electricity! Batteries, insulators, electromagnetic currents, batteries can withstand a lot of incoming charges, the more powerful the battery, the bigger the charger can withstand, but if the incoming charge exceeds the outgoing charge, the battery might EXPLODE!|Dr. [[Jallings]]|The Amazing Spider-Man 2}} After his battle with [[Electro]] in {{WPS|Times Square|Times Square}}, Peter listens to [[Gone, Gone, Gone (song)|''Gone, Gone, Gone'']] whilst decorating his room with pictures of his parent's death and Gwen. Later, he realizes he has to upgrade his webs in his [[Web-Shooters|web shooters]] to keep Max from frying his butt off. He decides to test them in the basement. [[File:Screenshot (1176).png|thumb|251x251px|Parker modifiying his [[Web-Shooters|Web-Shooters']] batteries]] He ends up testing his webs with his old web shooters. He hooks them up to a battery and he shocks them. They explode time after time. One web even catches fire, which causes Peter to scream in panic, for fear of burning down the house. Additionally, O'Hara had previously mentioned how the [[Spider-Man Multiversal Crisis|incident]] that occurred in [[List of Alternate Universes|Earth-199999]] which was caused by [[Doctor Strange]] and Spider-Man caused a turbulence within the [[Multiverse#Spider-Verse|Spider-Verse]], an incident that '''Peter''' participated in.As depicted in [[Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse|''Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse'']] == Future == ''To be added'' ==Personality== [[File:Static-assets-upload4863987930643057212.webp|thumb|Peter Parker was an intelligent yet sarcastic individual, and had hard times moving on following the deaths of certain people such as [[George Stacy]], [[Gwendolyne Stacy]] and his uncle [[Ben Parker]].]]{{Quote|I couldn't save her. I'm never going to be able to forgive myself for that.|Spider-Man|wikipedia:Spider-Man: No Way Home}} Peter Parker had all the qualities of a 17-year old crimefighter; shy, smart, broody, aggressive, sarcastic, troubled, awkward, and intelligent. Peter never truly got over his parents leaving him and then dying in an unfortunate plane crash, he possesses a scientific curiosity and is considered something of a geek at his school which made him a target for the local bully, Flash Thompson. This incarnation of Peter put more emphasis on the angst that a teenager would have. Peter had a tendency to use humor as a defensive mechanic, a trait that was only amplified when he became Spider-Man using sarcasm and wittiness before and during his fight with criminals. However behind this naturally upbeat demeanor Peter also possesses a tragic and lonely side, brought on by the immense guilt of the deaths of the people he held close, most specifically his Uncle Ben who's killer Peter met and actually let walk free simply to spite the owner of a convenience store. Another contributing factor for his guilt was his crush, Gwen Stacy's father dying at the hands of The Lizard while trying to save Spider-Man and with his dying breath he asks that Peter keep his daughter out of his vigilante activities which he fails to honor and this causes PTSD and even hallucinations. What proved to be a final straw for Peter was the woman he loved, Gwen Stacy died in his arms during a fight with The Green Goblin and for a few months he hung up being Spider-Man but eventually returned when the city was under threat.[[File:Peter Parker.png|left|thumb|Parker was regarded as one of the best in [[Midtown Science High School]]]] Peter also had a vengeful side to him, specifically before he truly become Spider-Man he used his newfound powers to search for the criminal who killed his Uncle Ben and presumably planned to return the favor however it quickly evolved into something more righteous. This was also shown when he humiliated the bully Flash in front of his entire basketball team. After getting his newfound powers form Oscorp, Peter had difficulty controlling them and was unintentionally destructive which was shown when he destroyed his bathroom, a football goal, a basketball net and the front door. He also took his time to torment a car thief that he thought could've killed his uncle, and even intended to kill him at one point even though he didn't need to. Peter was also something of a poor liar, every night he would return home with different scars and would tell a ridiculous story to cover it up. Peter eventually became obsessed over finding out what happened to his parents.[[File:Peter Three Infobox.jpg|thumb|Peter could not have the ability to forgive himself after losing Gwen.]]Although he retained most of his overall qualities, Peter was shown to be immensely depressed over his failure to save Gwen, even hinting that he resorted to killing criminals in his bitterness and rage. He also showed to be self-loathing, to the point of calling himself lamer than his other counterparts, having fought Rhino while the other two both fought aliens. However, this changed when his older variant repeatedly told him that he was amazing and not to have a low opinion on himself. After saving MJ, Peter finally overcame his depression due to redeeming himself for not being able to save Gwen. When he returned to his universe, he was notably more happy and content with himself. ==Powers and Abilities== {{Quote|He was bitten by one of those [[Araneus Oscorpeus|things]], and it worked. I don't know [[Transformation of Peter Parker|how]], and I don't know why, but... he can do everything else a spider can, including self-heal.|[[Green Goblin|Harry Osborn]]|The Amazing Spider-Man 2}} ===Powers=== {{Quote|Nice arm."
"It's just the wrist. It's just all in the wrist, buddy.|Peter Parker and [[Green Goblin|Harry Osborn]]|The Amazing Spider-Man 2}} [[File:Asmcap30.png|thumb|250px|Spider-Man holding back a 37,000lb bus.]]'''Spider Physiology''': After being bitten by a genetically engineered spider, Peter Parker developed a number of superhuman abilities; the bite triggered numerous body-wide changes within Parker, granting him superhuman strength, speed and agility, toughened flesh, the ability to adhere to surfaces and a supernatural spider-sense; *'''Superhuman Strength:''' As a result of the spider's bite, Peter possesses the proportionate strength of a spider; affording him considerable superhuman strength, far superior to that of any ordinary Human. Peter's muscles are stronger and more efficient; he is shown to be able to easily overpower, lift, and toss normal humans, pull apart guns, destroy his alarm clock by merely hitting it, shattering the backboard of a basketball hoop at school, and doing the same to his front door glass panel when he slammed the door shut, swing at high velocities on a rope or web, hold a van with one hand while hanging from a web, throw a football hard enough to dent a goalpost, exert enough force to prevent a bus from crashing into two bystanders, rip a fire hydrant out of the ground with his webbing, and catch a falling police car (as well as other cars with his webbing). He has restrained the [[Lizard]], swing around and weaponize a manhole cover, and poke a hole in his own webbing (which is 10 times the tensile strength of steel and could pull jets). His strength also allows him to jump higher and farther than a normal human, easily capable of jumping from one building over a street to the next. Spider-Man's strength is great enough to lift 18.5 tons.[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iO0ymtLP9wQ "How Strong is the Amazing Spider-Man?" - YouTube] [[File:Spider-ManInShock.jpg|thumb|250px|Spider-Man withstanding Electro's attacks.]] *'''Superhuman Durability:''' Peter's body is physically tougher and more resistant to injury than the body of a normal human; after being affected by the modified spider bite, peter’s muscles and flesh had become tougher and stronger. Peter has endured falls from hundreds of feet in the air, slash wounds to the chest, gunshot wounds, the shock from a taser, high voltage electric shocks, as well as high velocity impacts such as being smashed into brick walls, with only minimal injuries and discomfort. *'''Superhuman Speed:''' Peter is able to run and move at superhuman speeds, faster than any regular human; on-foot he can easily outpace normal humans, and keep up with accelerating vehicles, however he is able to better traverse by web-swinging. Peter was able to quickly take down multiple armed officers in a matter of seconds, and outmanoeuvre other enhanced individuals; such as the [[Lizard]] and [[Green Goblin|Harry Osborn]]. *'''Superhuman Agility:''' Peter’s agility, balance, and bodily coordination are enhanced to tremendous superhuman levels; he is able to easily run, move, jump, and climb, as well as perform advanced physical acrobatic and gymnastic feats. Peter’s agility, flexibility, and acrobatic prowess is superior to even the most accomplished circus aerialists and acrobats, being akin to that of a spider; he is able to perform high jumps, somersaults, and flips with ease. *'''Superhuman Stamina:''' Peter's advanced musculature produces fewer fatigue toxins during physical activity than an ordinary human; he is able to exert himself in a superhuman capacity, and for considerably longer than a normal Human. Peter is also able to continue to function, despite considerable injury; such as when he had been shot in the leg, and had to swing across the city in order to engage the lizard in combat. {{Quote|Good reflexes.|[[Lizard|Curt Connors]]|The Amazing Spider-Man}} [[File:SpiderManDodgesBullet.jpg|thumb|250px|Spider-Man dodging a bullet at close range.]] *'''Superhuman Reflexes:''' Peter's reflexes operate so fast that with the assistance of his Spider-Sense, he is able to easily evade gunfire even from close range. His reflexes are roughly 35 times faster than that of a regular human. {{Quote|Just shake it off, it's just your bones, your muscles, and your organs.|Spider-Man, recovering from his battle against Electro|The Amazing Spider-Man 2}} *'''Accelerated healing factor:''' Peter's superhuman metabolism allows him to rapidly heal faster than normal humans. He may also be more immune to toxins and alcohol and drugs or could rapidly lose their effects quicker. It is unknown if his healing is fast enough to affect his aging. He healed from the slashes that were on his chest and a gunshot wound to his leg without medical attention. *'''Superhuman senses:''' Peter's senses appear to be heightened, especially when used in conjunction with his spider-sense. However, his sight appeared to be unchanged after the spider bite, as he still had to use his glasses, he later presumably stops wearing contacts throughout most of the first film. However, he has used vibrations to sense potential enemies much like most real spiders. **'''Superhuman Hearing:''' Peter is able to hear any danger, via spider sense well he will hear that it also may warn him of any danger. ** '''Superhuman Balance:''' Peter is able to perfectly balance on any object no matter how small or narrow with little effort even with two fingers. **'''Spider-Sense:''' A tingling sensation in Peter's skull warns him of danger, where it is coming from and how to avoid it noted by a subtle "thunk" that sounds like a combination of someone hitting a bass drum once immediately followed by a high pitched bell ringing sound. It seems likely that the stronger the tingling, the more imminent and threatening the danger is and that Peter can ignore it through intense concentration, and if he is exhausted or distracted it can lose some effectiveness. This power is passive and not fully controlled by Peter. His Spider-Sense is stronger than his counterparts, as it was able to detect danger from long distance’s as it detected Max in Times Square when he was absorbing electricity. [[File:Amazing Spider-man 07.jpg|thumb|250px|Parker glimpsing his new powers.]] *'''Wallcrawling:''' Although not explained at all, it's most likely like his mainstream and Ultimate counterparts where Spider-Man's exposure to the mutated spider venom induced a cerebellum-wide alteration of his engrams resulting in the ability to mentally control the flux of inter-atomic attraction between molecular boundary layers. This overcomes the outer electron shell's normal behavior of mutual repulsion with other outer electron shells and permits the tremendous potential for electron attraction to prevail. The mentally controlled sub-atomic particle responsible for this has yet to be identified. This ability to affect the attraction between surfaces is so far limited to Spider-Man's body and another object, with an upper limit of several tons per finger. This ability appears to be consciously used. Although can be activated by stress or altered states of consciousness as seen twice when he awakens from slumber. ===Abilities=== {{Quote|He's one of [[Midtown Science High School|Midtown Science]]'s best and brightest.|[[Gwendolyne Stacy]]|The Amazing Spider-Man}} *'''Genius-Level Intelligence:''' Peter is very smart, specifically in engineering, chemistry and physics. His uncle Ben, of his own admission, stopped being able to help him with his homework when he was 10. He is smart enough to design a computer-activated door lock, perfect his father's webbing formula and to construct working mechanical web-shooters. He also apparently solves a formula that is key to creating the formula that mutates Curt Connors into the Lizard. He also had a perfectly-solved Rubik's Cube on his desk. * '''Expert Combatant:''' Using a mixture of his superhuman strength, speed, agility, reflexes, equilibrium, Spider-Sense, and Web-Shooters, Peter is able to utilize a devastating acrobatic fighting style that makes him a formidable opponent and allowed him to easily take down thugs, cops and even the more physically powerful [[Lizard]]. His fighting style also appears to incorporate professional wrestling techniques, as seen when he used a Frankensteiner on a car thief. {{Quote|I'm swinging here!|Spider-Man|The Amazing Spider-Man}} * '''Master Acrobat:''' Due to Peter's superhuman physical powers, he easily surpasses normal acrobats as he is able to perform somersaults, flips, spins, and cartwheels effortlessly. *'''Skateboarding:''' Before the genetically-altered spider bite, Peter is a very skilled skateboarder and skated everywhere he went. [[File:Spider-Man-TASM2.png|thumb|Spider-Man's second suit]] ==Equipment== *'''[[Web-Shooters]]:''' After getting his powers, Peter mysteriously bought Oscorp's Bio-Cable and incorporated the cartridges into his homemade web-shooters. **'''Web Fluid:''' The web fluid, developed by Oscorp, is a shear-thinning liquid, virtually solid until a shearing force is applied to it, rendering it fluid, whose exact formula remains unknown, but originated from the very enhanced spiders that bit him. The web line’s tensile strength is estimated to be 120 pounds per square millimeter of a cross-section. *'''[[Spider-Man Suit|Spider-Man suit]]:''' Using his knitting skills, Peter crafts a suit, made from mostly spandex, that not only allowed him to be mobile, but also allowed him to keep his identity hidden. However, since the suit is mostly made from spandex, it does not offer much in terms of protection as it can easily be torn by any type of sharp object that comes in contact with it. == Relationships == {{Section stub}} {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} === Family === *[[Mary Parker]] † - Mother *[[Richard Parker]] - Father *[[Ben Parker]] † - Uncle * [[May Parker]] - Aunt === Allies === *[[Midtown Science High School]] - Former Classmates **[[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen Stacy]] † - Former Classmate and Girlfriend **[[Flash Thompson]] - Former Bully, Former Enemy and Fan **[[Missy Kallenback]] - Admirer **[[Sally Avril]] *[[Lizard|Curt Connors/Lizard]] - Temporary Ally and Attempted Killer *[[Lizard/Variant Lizard|Curt Connors/Lizard]] *[[George Stacy]] *[[Electro/Variant Electro|Max Dillion/Electro]] - Fan and Former Attempted Killer *Ned's Lola *[[Michelle Jones]] *[[Ned Leeds]] *[[Spider-Men]] - Teammates **[[Spider-Man/Sacred Marvel|Peter Parker/Spider-Man]] - Alternate Self **[[Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man|Peter Parker/Spider-Man]] - Alternate Self *[[Flint Marko/Peter-Two|Flint Marko/Sandman]] - Situational Enemy * [[Doctor Octopus|Otto Octavius/Doctor Octopus]] - Former Enemy *[[The Green Goblin|Norman Osborn/Green Goblin]] - Attempted Killer *[[Doctor Strange|Stephen Strange/Doctor Strange]] *[[Mary Jane Watson|Mary Jane Watson]] {{Col-2}} === Enemies === *[[Oscorp Industries]] *[[Rodrigo Guevara]] - Impersonating Victim *[[Dennis Carradine]] *[[Electro|Max Dillion/Electro]] - Former Fan and Attempted Killer *[[Oscorp Industries|Oscorp]] - Former Allies *[[Green Goblin|Harry Osborn/Green Goblin]] - Former Best Friend, and Attempted Killer *[[The Rhino|Aleksei Sytsevich/Rhino]] - Attempted Killer *[[Norman Osborn|Norman Osborn]] † {{Col-end}} ==Trivia== *''"With great power comes great responsibility"'' is a very famous quote from the ''Spider-Man'' franchise, first introduced by the narrator in Spider-Man's debut issue of ''Amazing Fantasy''. This line is often quoted in the video game but is not heard in the film. * Only four people know Spider-Man's identity; [[Lizard|Curt Connors]], [[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen Stacy]], [[George Stacy]], and [[Green Goblin|Harry Osborn]]. **Ironically these people have either died over the course of the film series or are (currently) imprisoned. *Parker is a fan of the film ''{{WPS|Rear Window|Rear Window}}'', as seen by a poster in his room. ==Behind the scenes== *The Spider-Man suit that Peter wears in the first movie and game is unlike any other ''Spider-Man'' suit seen before, although similar to his classic red and blue. **In ''The Amazing Spider-Man'', Spider-Man wears a suit made especially for the film that includes [[Web-Shooters]] that are not modeled on any previous design. **Marc Webb has stated that Spider-Man's suit in ''[[The Amazing Spider-Man 2]]'' will have various aesthetic changes to make the suit look similar to the original ''Spider-Man'' suit, including "making the eyes bigger".{{Ref|first=|last=|date=November 14, 2012|url=http://www.aceshowbiz.com/news/view/00055489.html|title=Andrew Garfield Talks Costume Change and Exploring Peter Parker in 'Amazing Spider-Man 2'|website=Ace Showbiz|retrieved=November 17, 2012}} ***In February 2013, Webb revealed Spider-Man's new suit. *In February 2015, it was revealed that Andrew Garfield was dropped from the role of Spider-Man in favor for another reboot as Sony are collaborating with Marvel Studios to involve the character in the [[w:c:marvelcinematicuniverse:Marvel Cinematic Universe|Marvel Cinematic Universe]] with [[Tom Holland]] as the new [[Spider-Man/Sacred Marvel|Spider-Man]]. *[https://www.logaster.com/blog/spiderman-logo/ Spider-Man's first symbol] was created in 1966 by John Romita. Since then, it changes a lot of times. However, the designers returned to his hero mascot natural shape. '''Trivia for ''{{WPS|Spider-Man: No Way Home|Spider-Man: No Way Home}}:''''' *Amazing Spider-Man in the Marvel Cinematic Universe continues the live-action incarnation of the character from [[Marc Webb]]'s [[The Amazing Spider-Man (series)|''The Amazing Spider-Man'' duology]] from [[Sony Pictures Studios]], who was portrayed by Andrew Garfield and by Max Charles when he was younger. * There were plans for the animated film ''{{WPS|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}}'' to include this Spider-Man in a scene, which would have featured him alongside [[Tobey Maguire]] and [[Tom Holland]]'s [[Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man|versions]] [[Spider-Man/Sacred Marvel|of Spider-Man]], but the scene was scrapped due to fears it would have been too convoluted. *To differentiate Amazing Spider-Man from his alternate counterparts in the ''Spider-Man: No Way Home'' screenplay, Chris McKenna and Erik Sommers named him "Webb-verse Peter" and "Webb-verse Spider-Man" in honor of Marc Webb when writing his dialogue. *It was Jon Watts' idea to have Amazing Spider-Man rescuing Michelle Jones from falling to her death during the Battle at Liberty Island as a callback to how he failed to save his girlfriend [[Gwendolyne Stacy|Gwen Stacy]] in ''[[The Amazing Spider-Man 2]]''. *William R. Spencer was a stunt double for Andrew Garfield in the role of Spider-Man. *A.J. Danna was a stand-in for Andrew Garfield in the role of Spider-Man. ==Notes== ==References== {{Gallery|Spider-Man/Gallery}} {{Wq|Spider-Man}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{WP}} *{{WP|Peter Parker (The Amazing Spider-Man film series)}} *{{Marvel}} *{{Marvel|Peter Parker (Earth-120703)|Spider-Man}} *{{Wiki|Amazing Spider-Man|Amazing Spider-Man|marvelcinematicuniverse|Marvel Cinematic Universe}} [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Male]] [[Category:Heroes]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:The Amazing Spider-Man characters]] [[Category:The Amazing Spider-Man 2 characters]] [[Category:The Amazing Spider-Man (video game) Characters]] [[Category:The Amazing Spider-Man 2 (video game) Characters]] [[Category:Brown Hair]] [[Category:Brown Eyes]] [[es:El Hombre Araña]] [[ru:Питер Паркер]]
